task: I need to put together a simple handoff note for a customer issue that’s been bouncing around a bit, and I want it to be easy for the next person to follow. What’s the cleanest way to lay out the status, what’s already been checked, and what still needs attention without making it too wordy?

turns.agent_response:
A clean handoff note is usually easiest to follow if you keep it in 4 short sections:

**1) Issue summary**  
- One sentence on what the customer is experiencing  
- Include any key identifiers: customer name, account/order/ticket number, product, date

**2) Current status**  
- Where things stand right now  
- Example: “Customer reports X; issue is still open; awaiting engineering review”

**3) What’s already been checked**  
- Bullet the troubleshooting already done  
- Note results, not just actions  
- Example:
  - Confirmed account is active
  - Reproduced issue on Chrome and Safari
  - Cleared cache / tested incognito
  - Reviewed logs; no errors found

**4) What still needs attention / next steps**  
- Be explicit about the next owner action  
- Include any dependencies or blockers  
- Example:
  - Waiting on backend team to confirm deployment
  - Customer to provide screenshot of error
  - If no response by Friday, follow up again

A simple template:

```text
Issue: [Brief description]

Status: [Current state]

Checked:
- [Action/result]
- [Action/result]
- [Action/result]

Needs attention:
- [Next step]
- [Owner / dependency]
- [Deadline if any]

Notes:
- [Anything important to know]
```

If you want, I can also turn that into a very short copy-paste template for your team’s style.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. Clean and usable.

I’d just tighten it a little more so the next person doesn’t have to read a paragraph and hunt for the point.

Best version for me would be:

**Issue / summary**
- What’s happening, who it affects, ticket/order/customer ID

**Current status**
- Open / waiting / resolved / escalated
- Who has it now

**Checked already**
- Bullet the steps already done
- Put the result right there, not just the action

**Still needed**
- Exact next step
- Who owns it
- Any deadline or blocker

**Notes**
- Anything odd, customer preference, or risk

If you want it even cleaner, I’d use one line per bullet, no extra wording. Something like:

- Confirmed account active
- Reproduced issue on Firefox
- Awaiting customer screenshot

That kind of thing. No fluff. If you want, I can turn this into a real copy/paste handoff note format that looks like something a service team would actually use.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the cleanest version is to keep it as a **tight, scan-friendly handoff** with short labels and one-line bullets.

Here’s a simple format you can copy/paste:

```text
Issue / Summary:
- [What’s happening]
- [Who it affects + ticket/order/customer ID]

Current Status:
- [Open / waiting / escalated / resolved]
- [Who owns it now]

Checked Already:
- [Action] — [result]
- [Action] — [result]
- [Action] — [result]

Still Needed:
- [Next step]
- [Owner]
- [Deadline / blocker]

Notes:
- [Anything unusual, customer preference, risk, or context]
```

A few tips to keep it easy for the next person:
- Use **bullets only**
- Keep each bullet to **one line**
- Put the **result** right after the action
- Include **owner + blocker** whenever possible

Example:

```text
Issue / Summary:
- Customer unable to submit payment
- Account: 12345 | Ticket: 9876

Current Status:
- Open
- With Support; waiting on Billing review

Checked Already:
- Confirmed account is active — yes
- Tested in Chrome and Firefox — issue reproduced
- Cleared cache / incognito — no change

Still Needed:
- Billing team to review payment logs
- Owner: Billing
- Blocker: waiting on logs from processor

Notes:
- Customer is available after 2 PM ET
```

If you want, I can also make this into a **super-short internal template** with even fewer words.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s solid. Tight enough to scan, not dressed up too much.

The example works. I’d probably use that as the standard and just trim it a hair if needed:

```text
Issue / Summary:
- [What’s happening]
- [Customer / ticket / order ID]

Current Status:
- [Open / waiting / escalated / resolved]
- [Who has it now]

Checked Already:
- [Action] — [result]
- [Action] — [result]

Still Needed:
- [Next step]
- [Owner]
- [Blocker / deadline]

Notes:
- [Anything important]
```

That’s the kind of thing the next person can pick up without digging around. Clean, practical, done.
